description: Cherry-picking commits that a later merge also carries over produces duplicate commits and confusing divergence between branches.
body: Failure mode: a commit is cherry-picked to another branch and the source branch is later merged into the same target, so the same change lands twice with different hashes. Detection signals: git log shows two commits with identical diffs; a merge raises conflicts on changes that look already applied; release notes list a fix twice. Do not cherry-pick a change that an upcoming merge will also bring. Safer fallback: prefer merging when the whole branch will integrate, reserve cherry-pick for isolated hotfixes, use -x to record origin, and plan how the divergence resolves.
